rationale: This structure is organized around the three pillars of the field: core Chemical Engineering, core Bioengineering, and the crucial interface between them. Core ChemE is represented by Process Systems, Catalysis, and Separations. The Bioengineering side is broken into its major, practitioner-recognized subfields like Biomaterials, Tissue Engineering, and Biomechanics. The highly active interface is captured by categories like Bioprocess Engineering, Drug Delivery, and Synthetic Biology. I deliberately combined 'Tissue Engineering' with 'Regenerative Medicine' as they are inextricably linked in modern research. I also kept 'Polymer & Materials Engineering' as a single category focused on the ChemE perspective, rather than splitting it, to avoid over-specialization and potential overlap with a separate Materials Science L1 category.

subspecialties.name: Process Systems Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Design, optimization, and control of large-scale chemical processes.
covers: Process modeling, simulation, and control
Supply chain optimization, process safety, plant design
explicitly_excludes: Reactor design focus → Catalysis & Reaction Engineering
Bioreactor systems → Bioprocess Engineering

subspecialties.name: Catalysis & Reaction Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Developing catalysts and designing reactors for chemical transformations.
covers: Heterogeneous and homogeneous catalysis
Reactor design, chemical kinetics, transport phenomena in reactors
explicitly_excludes: Enzymatic catalysis → Bioprocess Engineering
Electrocatalysis for energy storage → Sustainable & Energy Systems

subspecialties.name: Separations Science
subspecialties.tagline: Purifying chemical and biological mixtures using physical and chemical means.
covers: Distillation, chromatography, membrane science
Adsorption, extraction, crystallization
explicitly_excludes: Downstream processing of biologics → Bioprocess Engineering

subspecialties.name: Polymer & Materials Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Synthesis, processing, and characterization of polymers and soft materials.
covers: Polymer chemistry and physics
Material processing, rheology, composites, functional materials
explicitly_excludes: Materials specifically for biological use → Biomaterials
Pure solid-state physics → Physics

subspecialties.name: Sustainable & Energy Systems
subspecialties.tagline: Engineering solutions for clean energy, storage, and environmental impact.
covers: Batteries, fuel cells, photovoltaics
Carbon capture, biofuels, life cycle assessment
explicitly_excludes: Traditional petrochemical processes → Process Systems Engineering
Biomass conversion in bioreactors → Bioprocess Engineering

subspecialties.name: Biomaterials
subspecialties.tagline: Designing materials that interact with biological systems.
covers: Biocompatible polymers, hydrogels, metallic and ceramic implants
Material-cell interactions, surface modification for biological response
explicitly_excludes: Using materials to grow tissues → Tissue Engineering & Regenerative Medicine
Formulations for drug release → Drug Delivery

subspecialties.name: Tissue Engineering & Regenerative Medicine
subspecialties.tagline: Building biological substitutes to restore or improve tissue function.
covers: Cell-scaffold constructs, stem cell differentiation
Organoids, 3D bioprinting, functional tissue development
explicitly_excludes: The material scaffold itself → Biomaterials
Genetic modification of cells → Systems & Synthetic Biology

subspecialties.name: Drug Delivery
subspecialties.tagline: Engineering vehicles and systems for targeted therapeutic delivery.
covers: Nanoparticles, liposomes, and hydrogels for drug release
Pharmacokinetics, controlled release mechanisms, targeted delivery
explicitly_excludes: Synthesis of the delivery polymer → Polymer & Materials Engineering
Discovery of the drug itself → Pharmacology

subspecialties.name: Biomechanics
subspecialties.tagline: Applying principles of mechanics to biological systems.
covers: Cellular mechanics, fluid dynamics in circulation
Orthopedic biomechanics, joint mechanics, mechanobiology
explicitly_excludes: Design of implant materials → Biomaterials
Design of active medical devices → Biomedical Devices & Instrumentation

subspecialties.name: Biomedical Devices & Instrumentation
subspecialties.tagline: Designing and building devices for diagnostics, therapy, and research.
covers: Biosensors, medical imaging hardware
Prosthetics, surgical tools, microfluidic diagnostic chips
explicitly_excludes: Clinical trials of devices → Medicine
Analysis of biological data from devices → Computational Biology & Bioinformatics

subspecialties.name: Systems & Synthetic Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Engineering biological parts, devices, and systems at the genetic level.
covers: Genetic circuit design, metabolic engineering
Genome editing tools (e.g., CRISPR), whole-cell modeling
explicitly_excludes: Large-scale cell culture for production → Bioprocess Engineering
Purely observational genomics → Biology

subspecialties.name: Bioprocess Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Using living cells or enzymes to produce valuable substances.
covers: Bioreactor design and scale-up
Fermentation, cell culture, downstream processing of biologics
explicitly_excludes: Genetic modification of the organism → Systems & Synthetic Biology
General chemical plant design → Process Systems Engineering

subspecialties.name: Computational Biology & Bioinformatics
subspecialties.tagline: Modeling and simulating complex biological systems and data.
covers: Metabolic network modeling, protein folding simulation
Molecular dynamics, systems pharmacology, bioinformatics tool development
explicitly_excludes: Process control simulation → Process Systems Engineering
Purely statistical analysis of clinical data → Medicine

subspecialties.name: Food Science & Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Applying engineering principles to food production, processing, and safety.
covers: Food processing operations, food rheology
Food preservation techniques, novel food product development
explicitly_excludes: General fermentation processes → Bioprocess Engineering
Nutrition and health studies → Medicine

uncovered_concerns: Thermodynamics & Transport Phenomena were considered but not included as a separate category, as they are foundational principles typically applied within one of the other subspecialties.
The 'Polymer & Materials Engineering' subspecialty could overlap with a future 'Materials Science' L1; its scope here is intentionally focused on the chemical engineering perspective.
'Computational Biology' is placed here due to its strong integration in BioE departments, but could also fit within a broader 'Computational Science' L1.
